topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box sat on the worn welcome mat like a discarded accusation. Liam dragged himself from the sofa, the movement heavy and reluctant. He stared at the plain brown package, the tape already peeling slightly at the edges. It was addressed to 'Apartment 3B,' a unit he hadn't seen in nearly six months. A low thrum of irritation vibrated in his chest. He didn't want it. He certainly didn't want any correspondence from the ghost of the last occupant. With a sigh that seemed to drain the remaining air from the room, Liam picked it up. His fingers brushed the rough paper, and he immediately felt a tightening in his jaw. He carried it to the kitchen counter, turning it over in his hands, the silence of the apartment amplifying the dull ache of resentment.

[story 2]

Elara found the delivery notification glaring on her phone screen. Another unsolicited item. She slumped onto the floor near the doorway, letting her head drop until her chin rested on her knees. The package, addressed to the former resident of the unit next door, looked utterly mundane—just a standard shipping box. A profound weariness settled over her, making the effort of even looking at it feel monumental. She didn't bother to open it immediately. Instead, she kicked a stray sock across the floor with unnecessary force. Every muscle in her body felt weighted down, a heavy cloak of dissatisfaction clinging to her. She just wanted it to vanish.

[story 3]

Mark stood by the front door, his shoulders slumped forward as he watched the delivery person drop the parcel onto the hallway table. He didn't move to retrieve it. His gaze remained fixed on the box, a deep furrow etched between his brows. He felt a distinct churning in his stomach, a familiar, unpleasant knot forming whenever he encountered something that wasn't entirely his own. He let out a slow, audible breath, the sound ragged in the quiet hallway. He picked up the package with two fingers, his grip tight enough that his knuckles went white, and without a word, he retreated into his apartment, the weight of the unknown item following him.
